titleStmt.title: Letter from James Davidson to Clara Davidson, 9 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: James Davidson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Captain James Davidson (1877-1916) to his mother Clara Mary Davidson informing her how he has spent the last few days of his leave. Davidson notes that the leave has done him a lot of good and hopes it will only be about three months before he is granted leave again.
This letter is part of a large correspondence from Captain James Davidson to his parents. Davidson, who was Managing Director of the Sirocco Engineering Works in Belfast, served with 13 Royal Irish Rifles and the Machine Gun Corps. His letters describe his experiences while training in England and serving in France. He was killed on 1 July 1916, the first day of the Battle of the Somme.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

correspAction.placeName: Jermyn Court Hotel, Piccadily Circus, Jermyn Street, London, England

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
JERMYN COURT HOTEL
PICCADILLY CIRCUS.
Main Entrance:- Jermyn Street, S.W. 9th April My dearest Mother This is another
lovely day & am going
motoring with a man
I know out Richmond
way. Leave is up
tomorrow. It has all
gone past so quickly
but it has been a delightful
little change & done me
a lot of good. Perhaps
the next leave will come
round sooner — it should
be in 3 months. Mrs Ducat dined with
me last night & we
went to see Romance.
It was one of the best
things I have seen for
a long time. The train
was awfully late arriving
on Sat. & I only had time
to go & order a pair of boots
at Faulkners & get one
or two other odds & ends
but will finish my shopping
tomorrow. The boots
will be sent to Seacourt &
then I will let you know
when to send them to me.
Will post a pair of shoes
home tomorrow before
leaving. With my fondest
love & hoping dear Mother
you will keep well while
I am away Your loving son Jim
Mrs. S.C. Davidson
Seacourt
Bangor
Co Down
Ireland 8-10 April 16
JERMYN COURT HOTEL
Piccadilly Circus.
Main Entrance:Jermyn Street,S.W
